summary: A biographical and philosophical overview of Martin Heidegger, focusing on his foundational work in phenomenology and hermeneutics, his lifelong preoccupation with 'Being', and his controversial involvement with the Nazi Party, which he later claimed to have navigated using double-speak regarding the confrontation between modern humanity and technology.

principles.statement: Philosophy must illuminate the a priori conditions that make entities intelligible to us.
principles.rationale: This ongoing project of transcendental hermeneutic phenomenology is necessary to understand Being and our relationship with it.
principles.source_quote: The project of illuminating the a priori conditions on the basis of which entities show up as intelligible to us is still at the heart of things.

principles.statement: The central crisis of the modern era is the confrontation between planetary technology and modern humanity.
principles.rationale: Heidegger viewed the 'essence of technology' as a defining and potentially overwhelming force in the modern world, which he claimed was the true 'movement' he was referring to in his controversial 1935 lecture.
principles.source_quote: the confrontation of planetary technology and modern humanity.

mental_models.name: Transcendental Hermeneutic Phenomenology
mental_models.description: A philosophical lens used to uncover and illuminate the foundational (a priori) conditions that allow entities to show up as intelligible to human beings.

signature_quotes.text: the confrontation of planetary technology and modern humanity.
signature_quotes.context: Added as a parenthetical clarification in his 1953 'Introduction to Metaphysics' to explain what he allegedly meant by the 'movement' in 1935.

signature_quotes.text: inner truth and greatness of this movement.
signature_quotes.context: From a 1935 lecture, which Heidegger later defended as double-speak referring to the essence of technology rather than praise for the Nazi Party.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on subjectivity to understand Being.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: In his later philosophy, Heidegger concluded that his earlier focus on 'Dasein' carried a subjectivity that 'ultimately blocks the path to an understanding of Being.'
